code-graph-endpoint.signature: GET /api/projects/{id}/code-graph -> { nodes: Array<{id, path, language, size}>, edges: Array<{source, target, type}>, total_files, total_edges }
code-graph-endpoint.description: Returns the full codebase dependency graph with file nodes and import/require edges

file-content-endpoint.description: Returns file content with path traversal protection. Rejects paths with '..', symlinks escaping project root, files >1MB, binary files

extended-codebase-service.description: Extends codebase_service.py to extract import/require statements and build file-to-file dependency graph. Supports .py, .js, .jsx, .ts, .tsx, .cjs, .mjs, .cts, .mts, .go, .rs

typescript-parser.description: Adds tree-sitter-typescript for accurate TypeScript parsing

sync-endpoints-only.description: All new endpoints calling tree-sitter must use sync 'def' (not 'async def') so FastAPI runs them in threadpool

path-traversal-protection.description: File content endpoint must validate resolved path with is_relative_to(project_path), reject '..' and symlinks

cache-bounded.description: _parse_cache must not grow unbounded; implement LRU eviction or size cap

definition.scope: Extend backend tree-sitter parsing to support full codebase graph generation with import/dependency extraction, new API endpoints, and missing file extension support.

tasks.description: Add .cjs, .mjs, .cts, .mts extensions to SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES in codebase_service.py
tasks.acceptance: RAPID's own .cjs files appear in codebase output

tasks.description: Add tree-sitter-typescript dependency and separate TypeScript language entry
Implement import/require extraction to build file-to-file edges

tasks.acceptance: Returns JSON with nodes, edges, total_files, total_edges

tasks.description: Create GET /api/projects/{id}/file endpoint with path traversal protection
tasks.acceptance: Valid paths return content; traversal attacks return 403/400

tasks.description: Add LRU eviction or size cap to _parse_cache
tasks.acceptance: Cache does not grow beyond limit
Default refetch interval is 30s+ instead of 2s

acceptance: All new endpoints return correct data for RAPID's own codebase
